consumption_context.action: Preserve upstream runtime blocked-tool-call claim boundary
consumption_context.purpose: Record downstream reliance without converting runtime blocking into a legal, safety, or correctness claim

consumed_claims.claim_text: The referenced runtime event record structurally represents a blocked tool-call event within the stated verification scope.

assurance_limitations: Software workflow logs may preserve useful event structure without asserting source truth.
A blocked event record does not by itself establish legal or policy sufficiency.

boundary_effect_detail.summary: The downstream record preserves the upstream claim boundary without adding a new deployment, legal, compliance, truth, or policy-correctness claim.

downstream_output.statement: The upstream claim boundary was consumed for evidentiary preservation only.

evidence_refs.description: CBC v0.2 example consumed by this CCE record.

cce_non_claims.statement: This CCE record does not assert that any consumed source claim is true.
This CCE record does not assert legal, contractual, regulatory, or compliance sufficiency.
This CCE record does not enforce, block, approve, or authorize runtime behavior.
This CCE record does not assert that all relevant upstream sources or context were captured.
